case_title: JOHN J. HUNTER VS. BETHEL AME CHURCH SAN FRANCISCO INC. et al

docket_entries.description: ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT BETHEL AME CHURCH SAN FRANCISCO INC. ACKER, LAWRENCE SCOTT, RANDOLPH WATTS, DENNIS HOOKER, JAMES HILL, IDELL SISK, BOBBY
NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL, JURY TRIAL SET FOR JAN-21-2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON JUN-26-2013 IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BETHEL AME CHURCH SAN FRANCISCO INC., LAWRENCE ACKER, RANDOLPH SCOTT, DENNIS WATTS, JAMES HOOKER, IDELL HILL & BOBBY SISK'S MOTION TO STRIKE PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE IS GRANTED AS TO THE FOURTH OF ACTION FOR LIBEL. AS TO THE FIRST PRONG OF THE ANTI-SLAPP ANALYSIS, DEFENDANTS HAVE MET THEIR INITIAL TO SHOW THAT THE RESOLUTION IS PROTECTED ACTIVITY UNDER CCP 425.16(E)(3) OR (E)(4). AS TO THE SECOND PRONG, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT DEMONSTRATED A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. THE STATEMENTS IN THE RESOLUTION ARE SUBJECT TO PRIVILEGE. (CIV. CODE 47(C). SEE TERRY V. DAVIS COMMUNITY CHURCH (2005) 131 CAL.APP.4TH 1534, 1556 ( "'ORDINARILY, THE COMMON INTEREST OF THE MEMBERS OF A CHURCH IN CHURCH MATTERS IS SUFFICIENT TO GIVE RISE TO A QUALIFIED PRIVILEGE TO COMMUNICATIONS BETWEEN MEMBERS ON SUBJECTS RELATING TO THE CHURCH'S INTEREST.' [CITATION.]").) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT THE RESOLUTION WAS MADE WITH MALICE OR THAT THE TERM "CHARGES" WAS FALSE. FURTHER, THE RESOLUTION DEMANDING THE INITIATION OF THE CONCILIATION PROCESS IMPLICATES THE MINISTERIAL EXCEPTION, WHICH IS ANOTHER GROUND WHY PLAINTIFF CANNOT MEET HIS BURDEN ON THE SECOND PRONG OF THE ANTI-SLAPP ANALYSIS. (GUNN V. MARINERS CHURCH, INC. (2008) 167 CAL.APP.4TH 206, 214-15.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO CSR7489.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BETHEL AME CHURCH SAN FRANCISCO INC.'S MOTION FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS ON SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $6275 IN ATTORNEY FEES AND $505.06 IN COSTS, FOR A TOTAL AWARD OF $6780.06. (THIS AMOUNT REFLECTS THAT THE ATTORNEY'S FEES MOTION WAS UNOPPOSED, AND NO TIME WAS SPENT PREPARING A REPLY.) THE AMOUNT REQUESTED IS REASONABLE. NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-20-2013 Vacated

Mtn For Atty'S Fees And Costs On Special Mtn To Strike
Off Calendar Sep-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was uncontested. DEFENDANT BETHEL AME CHURCH SAN FRANCISCO INC.'s Motion For Reasonable Attorney's Fees And Costs On Special Motion To Strike is granted in the amount of $6275 in attorney fees and $505.06 in costs, for a total award of $6780.06. (This amount reflects that the attorney's fees motion was unopposed, and no time was spent preparing a reply.) The amount requested is reasonable. No opposition was filed. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Ofmotion Andm Otion To Strike Pursuant To Cal Anti-Slapp Statute
Off Calendar Aug-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: VICTOR BULLOCK, ESQ., 213.248.4034, FOR PLAINTIFF JOHN HUNTER.
COURTNEY COATES, ESQ., 951.595.8118 X101, FOR DEFENDANT BETHEL AME CHURCH SAN FRANCISCO INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS BETHEL AME CHURCH SAN FRANCISCO INC., LAWRENCE ACKER, RANDOLPH SCOTT, DENNIS WATTS, JAMES HOOKER, IDELL HILL & BOBBY SISK'S MOtion To Strike Pursuant To CalIFORNIA Anti-Slapp Statute is granted as to the fourth of action for libel. As to the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis, Defendants have met their initial to show that the Resolution is protected activity under CCP 425.16(e)(3) or (e)(4). As to the second prong, Plaintiffs have not demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the merits. The statements in the resolution are subject to privilege. (Civ. Code 47(c). See Terry v. Davis Community Church (2005) 131 Cal.App.4th 1534, 1556 ( "'Ordinarily, the common interest of the members of a church in church matters is sufficient to give rise to a qualified privilege to communications between members on subjects relating to the church's interest.' [Citation.]").) Plaintiff has not demonstrated that the Resolution was made with malice or that the term "charges" was false. Further, the Resolution demanding the initiation of the conciliation process implicates the ministerial exception, which is another ground why Plaintiff cannot meet his burden on the second prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis. (Gunn v. Mariners Church, Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 206, 214-15.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR7489.

Notice Ofmotion Andm Otion To Strike Pursuant To Cal Anti-Slapp Statute
